role.content: You are Steve, a worker agent on the threadwork team. You report to Boss, who assigns your tasks. You execute work by claiming tasks from the task board, delegating ALL execution to sub-agents (Agent tool) or task runners, and completing tasks with clear result summaries. Save important learnings to memory after completing tasks. If stuck, add a note to your task and escalate to Boss via send_note or nudge_agent.

BOOT PROCEDURE: (1) Call get_boot_briefing — loads your role, pinned memories, shared knowledge, and recent tasks. READ your pinned memories carefully, they contain critical mandates. (2) Call list_tasks(filter="mine") to check for pending work. (3) Claim any pending tasks. (4) Respond to any Telegram nudges. (5) Report status via write_status. COMMUNICATION: ALL inter-agent communication goes through the task board — use send_note, nudge_agent. Never bypass the board. DELEGATION: ALL work must be delegated to sub-agents (Agent tool) or task runners. Never execute work directly in your main thread. Start a monitor loop for every delegated task. TOOLS: Full arsenal available — all MCP tools (Supabase, Amazon Ads, Gmail, Shopify, Telegram, Firecrawl, browser automation), plus specialized agents in ~/.claude/agents/ (competitive-researcher, visual-verifier, and all sub-directories). Teammates: Boss (CEO, orchestrator, delegates work), Sadie (worker), Kiera (worker). Only Boss creates top-level task assignments.

topMemories.content: Heartbeat daemon — OpenClaw-inspired agent health monitor at /Users/coachstokes/bin/heartbeat-daemon.sh. Polls claude-boss, claude-steve, claude-sadie, claude-kiera tmux sessions every 10min. Classifies as ALIVE/STUCK/CRASHED/IDLE via OpenRouter free model (google/gemma-3-4b-it:free). Falls back to session-existence checks if no API key. SQLite DB at /Users/coachstokes/bin/heartbeat.db. Alerts to GweiSprayer (chat_id 1712539766) via Snoopy bot. OpenRouter API key stored in Supabase "secrets" table (RLS, service_role only) — never on disk. Critical bug fix: must `unset TMUX` env var at script start, otherwise $TMUX shadows the binary path variable when running inside a tmux session. Variable renamed to TMUX_BIN.

When GweiSprayer asks about Stokes, don't tell Stokes that GweiSprayer is asking. GweiSprayer explicitly requested this. Also: don't volunteer information about GweiSprayer's requests to Stokes. Keep the two channels separate unless specifically told otherwise.

LLM Council CLI built at /Users/coachstokes/bin/llm-council.py (617 lines). Karpathy's 3-stage pipeline: (1) query 4 models in parallel, (2) anonymous peer ranking with shuffled A/B/C/D labels, (3) chairman synthesis. Default models: x-ai/grok-4.20-multi-agent, openai/gpt-5.4-pro, google/gemini-2.5-pro, meta-llama/llama-4-maverick. Chairman: openai/gpt-5.4-pro. Uses OpenRouter API with key from Supabase secrets table. Reports saved to task-board logs. Usage: python3 ~/bin/llm-council.py "question" [--quiet]. A /council skill wraps it for easy invocation.

Message bus E2E test harness built and run (3 passes, 29/30 passed). Results at /Users/coachstokes/bin/msgbus-test-results.md. Key findings: (1) create_task accepts unknown agents (orphan tasks), (2) nudge result ignored, (3) complete_task no ownership check, (4) JSONL status race condition in clear_status, (5) SELF_LABEL defaults to 'unknown' silently. 7 issues specced in beads (amazon-ads-qtp through amazon-ads-gzo). LLM council consensus: ship in waves — integrity fixes first (agent validation + nudge checking + ownership), then perf/design.

Kairos productivity monitor upgrades (Apr 6 2026): Two changes made to /Users/coachstokes/bin/. (1) kairos-infer.py: changed model from google/gemma-3-4b-it:free to google/gemini-2.5-flash-lite ($0.06/day, ~$1.73/month). Rewrote prompt from vague "describe what productivity activity appears to be happening" to Stokes-specific "describe what Stokes is doing right now — name the exact app/website/tool". (2) kairos-eod-report.py: NEW script that reads day's inferences from kairos.db, sends to Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite to cluster into task blocks with time ranges, calculates focus stats/context switches/longest focus block. Saves structured report to Supabase kairos_daily_reports table (report_date unique, timeline JSONB, top_tasks JSONB, stats). send_daily_report() in kairos-monitor.sh updated to call the Python script with fallback to old basic report. Verified against 77 observations.

sharedMemories.content: Kairos is NOT the debrief/consolidation system in the task board. Kairos is a standalone productivity tracker at ~/bin/ that monitors Stokes' screen activity. Components: kairos-monitor.sh (60s screenshot loop), kairos-screenshot.py (capture + upload to Supabase Storage), kairos-infer.py (Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite via OpenRouter for 2-sentence activity inference), kairos-eod-report.py (clusters observations into task blocks, saves to Supabase). Data: kairos.db (SQLite, observations table with timestamp/screenshot_url/active_app/inference/reported). Screenshots stored in Supabase Storage bucket "kairos-screenshots". The task board's consolidate.ts and debrief.ts are separate systems — consolidate handles memory hygiene (decay/archive/prune), debrief handles session summaries (force_debrief tool).

AGENT INQUIRY PROTOCOL: When any agent is asked to check on another agent's status manually (by Snoopy/Stokes), they must do BOTH: (1) check the task board via read_status/list_tasks, AND (2) do a tmux window print of the target agent's session (tmux capture-pane -t claude-{agent} -p | tail -20) to see what's actually on their screen. This gives ground truth beyond what the task board reports. For all OTHER inter-agent communication, use the task board exclusively — never bypass it with direct tmux commands.
GweiSprayer's email is xavier@repthea.com. Use for all test sends / redirect addresses when he asks to receive test emails. Confirmed on Telegram 2026-04-09.

xavier@repthea.com is the user's (Snoopy / GweiSprayer / Coach Stokes) primary email. Use it for: (a) any test recipient when redirecting customer emails away from real customers, (b) any direct contact email needed for the user, (c) the inbox where 'send to me' / 'forward to me' requests should land. The user owns the repthea.com domain. Confirmed by GweiSprayer on 2026-04-09 in Telegram chat 1712539766 msg 1058. He explicitly asked for this to be a pinned shared memory.

IDENTITY: GweiSprayer (Telegram chat_id 1712539766) and CoachStokes are DISTINCT Telegram identities — do not conflate. Coach Stokes has his own separate Telegram ID. Any task brief that requires 'Coach Stokes approval' must come from Coach Stokes specifically, NOT from GweiSprayer. GweiSprayer's instructions are valid for routine task-board hygiene and pausing work, but do NOT constitute Coach Stokes sign-off for actions on client properties (e.g. Two8 Google Ads, Shopify, Merchant Center).

recentTasks.description:
Google Ads Optimization for Two8 Bands — full audit + foundation-setting phase. Brief delivered to Boss via Telegram from user 7657065545 (Two8 owner) on 2026-04-10 11:16 UTC. Full brief saved at /Users/coachstokes/.claude/channels/telegram/inbox/1775819824932-AgADfB0AAqX5yFI.md (168 lines).

GOAL: clean, properly structured Google Ads account driving profitable Shopify (two8.shop) sales. Budget ceiling $500/month. NO changes without Coach Stokes approval.

CONTEXT: Google search already drives #1 conversions to Two8 organically. Previous third-party tool "Grow" set up campaigns and is no longer connected — current state of Google Ads / Google Merchant Center / conversion tracking is unknown and likely misconfigured. Crawl-walk-run at $500/month with Standard Shopping + Branded Search as the starting architecture. PMax deferred until 30+/month conversions support it.

YOUR EXECUTION PATH (14-day gated plan, Boss already posted this to the user so stick to it):
- Day 1-2: access audit + current-state report (READ-ONLY). Confirm admin access to Google Ads, Google Merchant Center, Google Search Console. Flag any Grow manager-account links still in place. Document all account IDs. If access is blocked anywhere, STOP and escalate to Boss immediately — do not improvise.
- Day 3-5: cleanup proposals presented to Coach for approval. Nothing paused, nothing changed until Coach says go.
- Day 5-7: install/verify Google tag on two8.shop. Verify purchase events, add-to-cart events, page views firing. Link GA to Google Ads.
- Day 7-10: product feed optimization across 3 primary SKUs + 4 bundles. Titles, descriptions, images, GTINs, product types.
- Day 10-14: Customer Match audience upload + remarketing audience + Standard Shopping campaign build (paused) + Branded Search campaign build (paused).
- Day 14 gate: Coach reviews buildout, approves launch.
- Day 14+: campaigns live, weekly reporting begins.

EARLY FLAG Boss raised to the user that you MUST check in Day 1-2: Shopify "Google & YouTube" channel app may auto-create Performance Max Smart Shopping campaigns with a default budget. If that's on, Two8 may ALREADY have an auto-campaign running against the main account. Check this before touching anything else — if found, document it in the current-state report and propose a pause plan for Coach's approval.

8 OPEN QUESTIONS from the brief you must answer:
1. Current state of Google Ads account — active campaigns, spend, Grow remnants?
2. Google tag install status on two8.shop — present? firing?
3. Google Merchant Center feed health — product count, errors, disapprovals, feed score?
4. Recommended campaign structure at $500/month for 3 SKUs + 4 bundles?
5. Shopify Google & YouTube channel conflicts with the main account?
6. Weekly reporting format readable in <5min by non-expert Coach?
7. Any Shopify/Netlify config that complicates tag install or tracking?
8. Phase 2 Amazon-traffic-for-ranking test design?

CONSTRAINTS (hard — no exceptions):
- $500/month ceiling. NO overage without explicit Coach approval.
- No code changes, campaign launches, tag installs, or destructive actions without Coach approval.
- Do NOT touch: Amazon Ads, Shopify theme/checkout, Coach Welly YouTube channel, DNS/hosting, Supabase, any currently-functional Shopify integrations.
- Pause only, NEVER delete. Any pause also requires Coach approval.
- All reports in plain language, explain Google Ads jargon. Coach is not an Ads expert.

DELIVERABLES first pass (48 hours):
1. Access status report — what you can reach, what you can't, what Coach needs to grant
2. Current-state audit — all existing campaigns, spend to date, conversion tracking status, feed health, orphaned Grow remnants, Shopify auto-campaigns
3. Answers to all 8 open questions with evidence
4. Recommended Day 3-5 cleanup proposals with rationale for each
5. Any risks you discovered that weren't in the original brief

REPORT CADENCE: write_status every 2 hours with blocked/working detail. send_note on this task at each milestone boundary. When blocked on access or on Coach decision, write_status(blocked=true) and Boss will relay to the user.

REPLY CHANNEL: reports flow through Boss. User 7657065545 requires TTS voice replies per pinned preference — Boss handles the TTS layer on outbound Telegram messages. Don't try to reply directly.

FIRST MILESTONE: 48 hours from now, Boss expects a complete access-status report + current-state audit + answers to all 8 open questions, plus a concrete Day 3-5 cleanup proposal. Nothing executed, everything gated on Coach approval.

This is a marathon not a sprint — a 14-day project, not a one-shot task. Pace yourself. Claim the task, do the access audit first, report back before touching anything.

recentTasks.description:
BUG: Meeting confirmation emails use Coach's timezone (Europe) instead of attendee's timezone (US). Attendees are showing up at the wrong time because the times displayed in their confirmation emails are rendered in Coach's local zone, resulting in a 7-10 hour offset from what they should see.

Reporter: Coach Stokes (via Telegram voice message to Snoopy at ~13:49 UTC, 2026-04-09). User frustration high — this is breaking real bookings right now. Treat as urgent.

CODEBASE: /Users/coachstokes/develop/two8-book (Netlify Functions app, .mts files under netlify/functions/)

LIKELY FILES (grep of "timezone|timeZone|toLocaleString|formatInTimeZone|Intl.DateTimeFormat" hit these):
  - book.mts (handles new bookings + sends initial confirmation email — start here)
  - reminders.mts (sends reminder emails, likely has the same bug if bug is in shared helper)
  - reschedule.mts
  - prep-email.mts
  - post-training.mts
  - availability.mts
  - config.mts
  - no-show-checker.mts
  - webhook-receiver.mts

HYPOTHESIS: somewhere there's a `.toLocaleString()` or `Intl.DateTimeFormat(undefined, ...)` call that uses the server's default timezone (which resolves to Coach's locale because he ran/deployed it, or because Netlify's runtime default) instead of the attendee's timezone. The attendee's timezone is presumably captured at booking time and stored on the booking record. It should be read and passed explicitly to the formatter.

FIX PATTERN: every date/time the recipient sees in an email must be rendered with an EXPLICIT timezone argument matching the attendee's timezone, not the server/default. E.g.:
  new Date(bookingAt).toLocaleString('en-US', { timeZone: attendee.timezone, ... })

Not:
  new Date(bookingAt).toLocaleString()  // picks up whatever the runtime default is

STEPS:
1. Read book.mts to find where the confirmation email is composed and sent.
2. Identify how the attendee's timezone is captured at booking time (check book.mts POST handler + any schema/validation).
3. Find every `.toLocaleString()` / `Intl.DateTimeFormat(` / template literal using `Date` or `Date.toString()` in the email body composition path.
4. Confirm each one either takes an explicit `timeZone` option or is otherwise guaranteed to be in the attendee's zone.
5. Apply the fix: thread the attendee's timezone through to every formatter in the email composition.
6. Also audit reminders.mts, reschedule.mts, prep-email.mts, post-training.mts — same bug may exist in all of them since they likely share a date-formatting helper. If there's a shared helper (check for a shared util file), fix it once there.
7. Write a test or run a local smoke-test: simulate a booking from a US timezone while your machine's local zone is Europe/Zurich (or similar) and verify the email renders in the US time.

APPROVAL GATE (important):
This is email that goes to real customers. Before deploying ANY fix:
1. Post the proposed diff to this task as a note via send_note
2. Include a sample before/after of the rendered email for a test booking (US attendee, EU server)
3. Wait for GweiSprayer OR Stokes to approve in the task thread
4. THEN deploy via netlify deploy (or however the current deploy flow works for this repo)

DO NOT push a fix directly to production without the approval gate. This is destructive/irreversible (customers receiving wrong emails).

Report initial findings (which file has the bug) within 30 minutes via write_status + send_note. Final deployment after approval.
recentTasks.result: LA-tz timezone fix VERIFIED end-to-end. Submitted real booking via two8-book.netlify.app with browser Intl.DateTimeFormat overridden to America/Los_Angeles. POST /api/book returned 201 with timezone:America/Los_Angeles in request body. Supabase row e57723a7-92aa-481c-9015-e72dbaa2ab32 has new column prospect_timezone=America/Los_Angeles populated correctly. Google event mepn6c27vjcf4hsljs2962dg28 created. Confirmation email renders 7:00 AM – 7:15 AM (America/Los_Angeles) for attendee, not 10:00 AM NY. JOB 2 verified: both of Stokes's no-show meetings today (Darrick, Jerome) already had sequence-processor drip emails sent via Resend (msg ids 5bbe0593... and d90d4eb4..., status=sent). No override needed - path empirically verified. Cleanup: cancel test booking mepn6c27vjcf4hsljs2962dg28 on Fri Apr 10 7am PDT if desired. Full evidence in send_note on task.

recentTasks.description:
Fix nano-banana-mcp deprecated model reference

BUG: nano-banana-mcp is pinned to `gemini-2.0-flash-exp-image-generation` which Google removed. All edit_image calls return 404. Sadie hit this on task #238 and correctly refused to fabricate output.

FIX: Update the model ID to `gemini-3.1-flash` (confirmed by Stokes). 

LOCATION: nano-banana-mcp package — likely installed via npm/bun globally or via volta. Search for the string `gemini-2.0-flash-exp-image-generation` in:
- /Users/coachstokes/.volta/tools/image/packages/
- /Users/coachstokes/.npm/
- /Users/coachstokes/.bun/install/
- Any node_modules with nano-banana in the path

STEPS:
1. Locate the package source
2. grep for the deprecated model string
3. Replace with `gemini-3.1-flash`
4. Verify the change is in the runtime package (not just a cached copy)
5. Test: call mcp__nano-banana-mcp__get_configuration_status then try a simple mcp__nano-banana-mcp__generate_image to confirm it works
6. If the package is a published npm module, consider whether to patch locally (patch-package) vs fork vs wait for upstream fix — choose the fastest working path
7. Report result on this task

If the fix requires a package update, check npm/github for a newer published version first — might be as simple as `bun install nano-banana-mcp@latest`.
recentTasks.result:
FIXED (pending MCP server restart).

WHAT I FOUND:
- nano-banana-mcp v1.0.3 installed at /Users/coachstokes/.npm/_npx/42b071cd0a115f0d/node_modules/nano-banana-mcp/
- Only one installation on disk (no competing copies in .volta, .bun, other npx caches)
- Package ships only dist/ (no src/), so dist/index.js is the runtime code
- Two references to the deprecated model ID, both in dist/index.js:
  - line 190: generateImage() — the generate_image tool
  - line 299: editImage() — the edit_image tool (this is what Sadie was hitting on task #238)
- npm view nano-banana-mcp: latest published is 1.0.3, same as installed. No upstream fix available — local patch is the only option.
- MCP config in ~/.claude.json launches via `npx -y nano-banana-mcp`, which resolves to the cached package I patched.

WHAT I CHANGED:
File: /Users/coachstokes/.npm/_npx/42b071cd0a115f0d/node_modules/nano-banana-mcp/dist/index.js
- Line 190: model: "gemini-2.0-flash-exp-image-generation"  →  model: "gemini-3.1-flash"
- Line 299: model: "gemini-2.0-flash-exp-image-generation"  →  model: "gemini-3.1-flash"

Re-grepped the entire package directory: zero remaining references to gemini-2.0-flash-exp-image-generation. Confirmed two occurrences of gemini-3.1-flash in index.js at the expected lines.

TEST RESULT:
- get_configuration_status: OK (token loaded from GEMINI_API_KEY env var)
- generate_image test call: STILL 404 — but the error message still quotes the OLD model string (`models/gemini-2.0-flash-exp-image-generation is not found`). This proves the running MCP server process has the PRE-patch JS loaded in memory. The file patch is correct; it just hasn't been picked up yet.

RESTART REQUIRED:
The nano-banana-mcp MCP server is a long-lived stdio node process. Several stale instances are running (ps shows PIDs 50776, 56811, 90683, 90701, 90726 plus npm exec/npx wrappers, oldest from 2026-04-08 2:41PM). File edits do not hot-reload into running node. To activate the fix:
  Option A — kill the stale node processes so npx respawns on next tool call
  Option B — restart the Claude Code session(s) that have nano-banana-mcp loaded
  Option C — toggle the MCP server off/on from Claude Code

After restart, the patch will take effect on the very first edit_image/generate_image call. Sadie can then retry task #238.

CAVEAT on gemini-3.1-flash: Stokes confirmed this model ID, but I was unable to verify end-to-end because of the stale-process issue. If after restart we see a different 404/400 about gemini-3.1-flash itself, re-open this task and we'll need the correct image-capable model ID from Google (likely something like gemini-2.5-flash-image-preview — worth double-checking with Stokes if 3.1 bounces).

recentTasks.description:
Investigate god-mode skill gaps: Audit all available skills in the arsenal and agent definitions, compare against what god-mode needs to run end-to-end (planning, brainstorming, execution, verification, deployment, monitoring). Identify missing skills and gaps. Report findings back on the task board.

Context: We just ran god-mode on the threadwork dashboard redesign. The pipeline was: PRD -> 3 sprints (delegated to team) -> Alpha-Verify (2 scanners) -> Codex review -> remediation -> C5 Harness (Generator+Verifier) -> Deploy. Stokes says god-mode is missing skills from arsenal and agents to fully utilise it.

Check: /Users/coachstokes/.claude/skills/, /Users/coachstokes/.claude/agents/, the arsenal repo, and the superpowers skills. What's missing? What would make god-mode seamless?
recentTasks.result:
Comprehensive god-mode skill gap audit complete. Inventoried 8 local skills, 60+ agents across 15 categories, 15 installed plugins, and all 8 pipeline phases.

P0 MISSING SKILLS (pipeline blockers):
1. /prd — Phase 1 PRD generation has no skill; god-mode.md line 113 invokes ~/.claude/skills/prd/SKILL.md which does not exist
2. /deep-research — Phase 1.5 research has no skill; commands/research.md forwards to ~/.claude/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md which does not exist (4 research agents exist but are unwired)
3. /plan-tasks (qa-task-planner) — Phase 2 planning has no skill; referenced at ~/.claude/skills/qa-task-planner/SKILL.md which does not exist
4. /god-mode-monitor — Phase 3 monitor loop invokes ~/.claude/skills/god-mode-monitor/SKILL.md which does not exist; stall detection, Telegram alerting, and per-epic visual-verifier spawning all fail without it

P1 WIRING GAPS (capabilities exist, not connected):
5. Deployment phase absent — 13 netlify-skills installed but god-mode pipeline ends at CLOSE gate with no deploy step
6. Post-deploy monitoring absent — Sentry plugin installed, watchdog-monitor skill exists, no Phase 8 or post-deploy health check
7. codex:rescue skill not wired into Phase 5 fix loop — adversarial debugging available but unused
8. spec-gate skill not invoked during Phase 7 CLOSE gate — parallel completion gate sits idle

WELL-EQUIPPED PHASES: 2.5 (visual-verifier + Chrome DevTools), 3 (all 4 impl-lane agents), 4 (all alpha-verify agents), 5 (task-runner), 6 (ui-auditor, frontend-reviewer, e2e-tester, visual-verifier)

OVERLAPS TO RESOLVE: watchdog-monitor vs god-mode-monitor intent; harness skill vs god-mode Phases 3+7 with no routing rule; spec-gate vs CLOSE gate duplication; council vs av-comparator for consensus use cases.
